channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Pikachu just helped deliver your pizza, Pokemon Go data is now guiding food delivery robots - Dexerto
item.description:
1. Pikachu just helped deliver your pizza, Pokemon Go data is now guiding food delivery robots  Dexerto
  2. How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world  MIT Technology Review
  3. ‘Pokémon Go’ players have been unknowingly training delivery robots  Popular Science
  4. Pokemon Go Had Players Capturing More Than They Realized  Hackaday
  5. 🇺🇸 Photos and scans from Pokémon Go players created a dataset of 30+ billion real-world images now being used to help delivery robots navigate streets without GPS. So yeah, people thought they were catching Pikachu. Turns out they were also training rob  x.com

item.title: I Installed the eufy S330 Video Doorbell Just as I Started a Whole-Home Remodel – And It Was a Lifesaver - Homes and Gardens
item.description: I Installed the eufy S330 Video Doorbell Just as I Started a Whole-Home Remodel – And It Was a Lifesaver  Homes and Gardens

item.title: NVIDIA says future GPUs will target 1,000,000x path tracing uplift over Pascal - VideoCardz.com
item.description:
1. NVIDIA says future GPUs will target 1,000,000x path tracing uplift over Pascal  VideoCardz.com
  2. DLSS 4.5 and Path Tracing Coming To 007 First Light, CONTROL Resonant, Tides of Annihilation & More. Plus, DLSS 4.5 Dynamic Multi Frame Gen Available March 31  NVIDIA
  3. Nvidia claims 1 million times better path tracing performance is coming in future gaming GPUs — says current GPUs are already 10,000x faster than Pascal  Tom's Hardware
  4. Nvidia’s DLSS 4.5 with 6x Frame Generation is rolling out at the end of March  The Verge
  5. Path tracing perfection might be Nvidia's future but the cure for shader stutter is coming much sooner  PC Gamer

item.title: CachyOS dethrones Arch as the top desktop distro for Linux gamers on ProtonDB - XDA
item.description: CachyOS dethrones Arch as the top desktop distro for Linux gamers on ProtonDB  XDA

item.title: I reviewed a $230, $120 and $80 mechanical keyboard this week — this is the only one I’d buy - Tom's Guide
item.description:
1. I reviewed a $230, $120 and $80 mechanical keyboard this week — this is the only one I’d buy  Tom's Guide
  2. Originally $220 Keychron OnePlus 81 Pro metal keyboard hits new Amazon all-time low at $123 (Save 44%)  9to5Toys
  3. Keychron just upgraded one of my favorite mechanical keyboards — but I’m still telling everyone to buy this cheaper model instead  MSN
  4. This budget Keychron mechanical keyboard is now down to its lowest price  XDA
  5. Keychron V1 Ultra 8K review: 8,000Hz polling and 660 hours of battery make it a winner  Tom's Guide

item.title: DR-DOS Is Back, But Not Quite As We Knew It - Hackaday
item.description:
1. DR-DOS Is Back, But Not Quite As We Knew It  Hackaday
  2. DR-DOS rises again – rebuilt from scratch, not open source  theregister.com
  3. ‘Clean-room reimplementation’ of DR-DOS hits early beta, modernizing the operating system 38 years after its debut — runs Doom, Warcraft, SimCity, and other period-appropriate titles  Tom's Hardware
  4. DR-DOS is coming back from the dead, rebuilt from scratch in pure assembly  TechSpot

item.title: Hands On: Razer's $1,337 Boomslang Throwback Is Absurd and Already Collector Bait - PCMag
item.description:
1. Hands On: Razer's $1,337 Boomslang Throwback Is Absurd and Already Collector Bait  PCMag
  2. I Tried the Razer Gaming Mouse That Costs $1,337  WIRED
  3. Razer's Boomslang is a $1,337 reminder of how bad things were before ergonomics  Yahoo Tech
  4. Razer Unveils $1,337 Boomslang Anniversary Mouse  findarticles.com
